data.narrative:
Skyroot Aerospace is a Series C-stage space launch company that has raised $95M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public-market Space & Aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-sector counterparts, with particular relevance to 5 same-stage private companies: Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $95M total raised proxy, yielding $162M. The Peer-Median method anchors to the median valuation of five Series C Space & Aerospace peers at $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue for the target company, though public sector comparables trade at a 59.8x P/S median.

The triangulated analysis across two applicable methods supports a range of $137M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $831M. The wide spread reflects tension between capital-raised progression and peer-group valuations, which skew materially higher. Key uncertainties include Skyroot's revenue traction relative to its peer cohort, technology differentiation in launch capability, and contract pipeline visibility—factors that would largely determine positioning within this $831M median-case estimate.
